# Real-time AI surveillance system design (2026-03-11 18:41Z)

_**User**_

Project Name: Visioryx

Tagline:
AI Powered Real-Time Face Recognition & Surveillance System

Developed by: Devender Vutukuru

You are a Senior AI Architect, Computer Vision Engineer, and Full-Stack Developer.

Design and build a **production-grade Real-Time AI Surveillance System** capable of:

• Face Recognition
• Unknown Face Detection
• Object Detection
• Multi-Camera Monitoring
• Real-Time Alerts
• Analytics Dashboard

The system must use **ONLY open-source technologies** and **no paid APIs**.

The final system must be scalable, modular, and enterprise-ready.

---

# 1. CORE FEATURES

The system must support:

Real-Time Face Recognition
Unknown Person Detection
Object Detection
Multi-Camera Monitoring
Face Registration System
Live Monitoring Dashboard
Detection Logging
Unknown Face Snapshot Storage
Object Detection Logging
Alert System
Face Clustering for Unknown People
Searchable Detection History
System Analytics

---

# 2. AI CAPABILITIES

Face recognition should include:

• Face detection
• Face alignment
• Face embedding generation
• Face similarity matching
• Unknown face classification
• Unknown face clustering

Matching should use **cosine similarity**.

Unknown faces should be stored and grouped by similarity so repeated unknown individuals can be identified.

---

# 3. TECHNOLOGY STACK

Backend

Python
FastAPI
OpenCV
NumPy
SQLAlchemy

Face Recognition

InsightFace (preferred)
OR face_recognition

Object Detection

YOLOv8 using Ultralytics

Frontend

Next.js
React
TypeScript
TailwindCSS

Admin Dashboard

Use the open source dashboard UI from:

minimals.cc

Database

PostgreSQL

Real-time updates

WebSockets

Containerization

Docker
Docker Compose

Optional acceleration

CUDA GPU support

---

# 4. SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

Camera Sources
↓
Frame Capture Service
↓
Frame Queue
↓
Face Detection
↓
Face Embedding Extraction
↓
Database Embedding Comparison
↓
Known / Unknown Classification
↓
Object Detection
↓
Event Logger
↓
WebSocket Event Dispatcher
↓
Admin Dashboard

Each camera must run in its own processing worker.

Use asynchronous pipelines.

---

---

# 7. FACE RECOGNITION PIPELINE

For each frame:

1 Capture frame
2 Detect faces
3 Align face
4 Generate embedding
5 Compare embedding with database
6 Compute cosine similarity
7 If similarity above threshold → Known user
8 Otherwise → Unknown
9 Draw bounding boxes
10 Send event to dashboard
11 Log detection

---

# 8. OBJECT DETECTION PIPELINE

Use YOLOv8.

Detect objects such as:

person
phone
laptop
bag
bottle
chair
car
motorcycle
bicycle

Bounding boxes must appear on the live feed.

Objects must also be logged in database.

---

# 9. ADMIN DASHBOARD

Build a modern dashboard using the **minimals.cc UI template**.

Dashboard pages:

Overview

Total Users
Active Cameras
Today's Detections
Unknown Detections

Live Monitoring

Live camera streams
Face bounding boxes
Detected names

Users

Register user
Upload face image
View users

Cameras

Add camera
Edit camera
Enable/disable camera

Detections

Detection history
Unknown faces
Filter by camera
Filter by date

Analytics

Detection trends
Unknown detection charts
Object detection charts

Alerts

Unknown face alerts
Security alerts

---

# 10. REAL TIME SYSTEM

Use WebSockets to push events to the dashboard.

Events

face_recognized
unknown_person_detected
object_detected
camera_status

Dashboard must update live without refresh.

---

# 11. PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION

Implement:

Frame skipping
Batch embedding comparison
GPU acceleration
Threaded camera workers
Async processing pipeline

System must support multiple cameras.

---

# 12. SECURITY

Implement:

JWT authentication
Admin login
Role based access

Roles

Admin
Operator

---

# 13. ALERT SYSTEM

Trigger alerts when:

Unknown person detected
Restricted object detected
Camera offline

Alerts must appear on dashboard.

---

# 14. DEPLOYMENT

Provide Docker setup.

Services:

Backend API
Frontend Dashboard
Database
AI worker

---

# 15. OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS

You must generate:

Complete code
File-by-file implementation
Installation commands
Database migrations
Docker deployment files
README documentation

Do NOT generate everything at once.

Start with:

Phase 1 — Project setup and architecture.

Wait for confirmation before continuing.

You can read the documentation and\nuse all of the MUI components here: [https://mui.com/components/](https://mui.com/components/).\n###### [](#3rd-party-libraries)🔸3rd party libraries\nWe include direct links to third-party used libraries:\n**Example:**\n* Carousel: [https://minimals.cc/components/extra/carousel/](https://minimals.cc/components/extra/carousel/).\n* Reference: [https://www.embla-carousel.com/](https://www.embla-carousel.com/).\n#### [](#feedback)Feedback\nWe are always open to your feedback at [[email&amp;#160;protected]](https://docs.minimals.cc/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#592a2c2929362b2d19343037303438352a773a3a662a2c3b333c3a2d64021430373034383504791f2b3634791a2c2a2d36343c2b)\nIf something is missing in the documentation, or if you found some part confusing, contact us with\nyour suggestions for improvement.\nWe love hearing from you!\n&gt;\n&gt; This project is just the user interface not including the backend and database.\n&gt;"
```json
}
```

Phase 1 is complete. Summary of what’s in place:

---

## Phase 1 — Project Setup & Architecture

### Project structure

```
Visioryx/
├── backend/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── api/           # auth, users, cameras, detections, analytics (placeholders)
│   │   ├── core/          # config, security, websocket_manager, logger
│   │   ├── ai/            # face_detector, face_embedding, face_matcher, etc. (placeholders)
│   │   ├── services/      # camera_worker, recognition_pipeline, etc. (placeholders)
│   │   ├── database/      # connection, models, migrations
│   │   └── main.py
│   ├── models/            # For InsightFace & YOLOv8 weights
│   ├── storage/           # registered_faces, unknown_faces, snapshots
│   ├── requirements.txt
│   ├── alembic.ini
│   └── .env.example
├── frontend/
│   └── src/
│       ├── app/           # dashboard, cameras, users, detections, analytics, alerts
│       ├── components/    # camera-stream
│       └── websocket/     # socket.ts
├── docker/
│   ├── Dockerfile.backend
│   ├── Dockerfile.frontend
│   └── docker-compose.yml
├── scripts/               # register_user.py, train_faces.py
├── .gitignore
└── README.md
```

### Implemented in Phase 1

| Component | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| **Core config** | Pydantic settings, env vars |
| **Security** | JWT, bcrypt, RBAC (Admin/Operator) |
| **Database** | SQLAlchemy async, PostgreSQL models |
| **Models** | `users`, `cameras`, `detections`, `objects`, `unknown_faces`, `alerts`, `auth_users` |
| **Migrations** | Alembic with initial schema |
| **WebSocket manager** | Connection handling and broadcast |
| **FastAPI app** | Health checks, CORS |
| **Frontend** | Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, page stubs |
| **Docker** | Backend, frontend, PostgreSQL compose |
| **README** | Installation and usage |

### Quick start

```bash
# Backend
cd backend && python -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env
alembic upgrade head
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000

# Frontend (new terminal)
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev
```

### Minimals.cc

Minimals.cc is a paid MUI template. The dashboard layout is set up so you can plug in the Minimal UI kit later. For now, the app uses MUI + Tailwind.

[nikh-iam/InsightFace-FaceRecognition](https://github.com/nikh-iam/InsightFace-FaceRecognition)<br/>3. [deepinsight/insightface](https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface)<br/>4. [pypi-insightface • tessl • Registry • Tessl](https://tessl.io/registry/tessl/pypi-insightface/files/docs/model-zoo.md)<br/>5. [pypi-insightface • tessl • Registry • Tessl](https://tessl.io/registry/tessl/pypi-insightface)<br/><br/># InsightFace Python Library for Face Detection and Embedding (2024)<br/><br/>InsightFace is a comprehensive Python toolkit for 2D and 3D face analysis that provides state-of-the-art algorithms for face detection, recognition, and alignment.[1][3]<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/><br/>The library offers:[1][5]<br/>- Real-time face detection and recognition<br/>- Face embedding generation for similarity matching<br/>- Facial landmark detection and alignment<br/>- Age and gender estimation<br/>- Face pose estimation<br/>- Face swapping capabilities<br/><br/>## Installation and Setup<br/><br/>InsightFace uses ONNX Runtime as its inference backend (starting from v0.2).[1] Installation is straightforward via pip:[1]<br/><br/>```python<br/>pip install insightface<br/>```<br/><br/>For GPU acceleration, you'll need to install `onnxruntime-gpu` separately.[1]<br/><br/>## Basic Usage<br/><br/>The primary interface is the `FaceAnalysis` class:[5]<br/><br/>```python<br/>from insightface.app import FaceAnalysis<br/>import cv2<br/><br/>app = FaceAnalysis(name='buffalo_l')<br/>app.prepare(ctx_id=0, det_size=(640, 640))<br/>img = cv2.imread('image.jpg')<br/>faces = app.get(img)<br/>```<br/><br/>This returns face objects containing embeddings, bounding boxes, landmarks, age, and gender.[5]<br/><br/>## Available Models<br/><br/>The library provides multiple pre-trained model packs with different accuracy/speed tradeoffs:[1]<br/>- **buffalo_l** (default): SCRFD detection + ResNet50 recognition, 326MB, 91.25% accuracy<br/>- **buffalo_m**: Similar accuracy to buffalo_l, more compact<br/>- **buffalo_s**: Smaller, faster model for resource-constrained environments<br/>- **antelopev2**: Highest accuracy option (407MB)<br/><br/>Models are available for non-commercial research purposes only.[1][3] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...](https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface/blob/master/python-package/insightface/app/face_analysis.py)<br/>2. [pypi-insightface • tessl • Registry • Tessl](https://tessl.io/registry/tessl/pypi-insightface/files/docs/face-analysis.md)<br/>3. [pypi-insightface • tessl • Registry • Tessl](https://tessl.io/registry/tessl/pypi-insightface/files/docs/model-zoo.md)<br/>4. [Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...](https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface/blob/master/examples/demo_analysis.py)<br/>5. [pypi-insightface • tessl • Registry • Tessl](https://tessl.io/registry/tessl/pypi-insightface)<br/><br/># InsightFace FaceAnalysis: Getting Faces, Bboxes, and Embeddings<br/><br/>## Basic Usage[5]<br/><br/>To use InsightFace's FaceAnalysis in Python:<br/><br/>```python<br/>from insightface.app import FaceAnalysis<br/>import cv2<br/><br/># Initialize and prepare the analyzer<br/>app = FaceAnalysis(name='buffalo_l')<br/>app.prepare(ctx_id=0, det_size=(640, 640))<br/><br/># Load image and detect faces<br/>img = cv2.imread('path/to/image.jpg')<br/>faces = app.get(img)<br/>```<br/><br/>## Accessing Results from Face Objects[2][5]<br/><br/>The `get()` method returns a list of `Face` objects containing:<br/><br/>**Bounding Box:**<br/>```python<br/>bbox = face.bbox.astype(int)  # Coordinates [x1, y1, x2, y2]<br/>```<br/><br/>**Embedding (Face Vector):**<br/>```python<br/>embedding = face.embedding  # Typically 512-dimensional vector<br/>```<br/><br/>**Additional Data:**<br/>```python<br/>det_score = face.det_score    # Detection confidence (0.0-1.0)<br/>kps = face.kps                # Facial keypoints, shape (5, 2)<br/>```<br/><br/>## Key Methods[2]<br/><br/>- **`FaceAnalysis.__init__(name='buffalo_l', root='~/.insightface', allowed_modules=None)`** - Initialize with model pack[2]<br/>- **`FaceAnalysis.prepare(ctx_id, det_thresh=0.5, det_size=(640, 640))`** - Prepare models for inference[2]<br/>- **`FaceAnalysis.get(img, max_num=0)`** - Detect and analyze faces, returns List[Face][2]<br/><br/>The Face object acts as a dictionary that dynamically accumulates attributes from different analysis models (detection, recognition, attributes).[2] |

</details>
</tool-use>

Summary of what was implemented:

---

## 1. material-kit-react

**https://github.com/minimal-ui-kit/material-kit-react** is useful and MIT-licensed. It uses Vite + React (not Next.js), so it’s used as a design reference. The dashboard layout (sidebar, cards, MUI) follows similar patterns. You can copy components from it into the Next.js app as needed.

---

## 2. Port-kill and start scripts

**macOS/Linux** – `scripts/kill-ports-and-start.sh`:
```bash
./scripts/kill-ports-and-start.sh all      # Kill 3000 & 8000, start both
./scripts/kill-ports-and-start.sh backend  # Backend only
./scripts/kill-ports-and-start.sh frontend # Frontend only
```

**Windows** – `scripts/kill-ports-and-start.ps1`:
```powershell
.\scripts\kill-ports-and-start.ps1 -Target all
```

These scripts kill processes on ports 3000 and 8000 before starting services.

---

## 3. Phase 2 & 3 implementation

### Backend APIs
- **Auth**: `POST /api/v1/auth/login`, `GET /api/v1/auth/me`
- **Users**: CRUD + face upload
- **Cameras**: CRUD
- **Detections**: List, filters, unknown faces, stats
- **Analytics**: Overview, detection trends, object stats
- **Alerts**: List, mark read
- **WebSocket**: `/ws` for real-time events

### AI modules
- **Face detector**: InsightFace (with OpenCV fallback)
- **Face embedding**: Extraction from images
- **Face matcher**: Cosine similarity
- **Object detector**: YOLOv8 (Ultralytics)
- **Unknown clustering**: Similarity-based grouping

### Services
- **Recognition pipeline**: Frame → detect → match → known/unknown
- **Camera worker**: Per-camera capture and processing

### Scripts
- `scripts/register_user.py` – Register user with face image
- `scripts/train_faces.py` – Batch extract embeddings
- `backend/scripts/seed_admin.py` – Create admin user

### Dashboard UI
- MUI layout with sidebar (Overview, Cameras, Users, Detections, Analytics, Alerts)
- Login page (`/login`)
- Overview page with stats (users, cameras, detections)

---

## Quick start

```bash
# 1. Backend
cd backend && python3 -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env
alembic upgrade head
python scripts/seed_admin.py
uvicorn app.main:app --port 8000 &

# 2. Frontend
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev

# Or use the kill-ports script:
./scripts/kill-ports-and-start.sh all
```

Then open http://localhost:3000, log in with `admin@visioryx.local` / `admin123`, and use the dashboard.

Summary of changes:

---

## Renamed script: `start-dev.sh`

- **Old:** `scripts/kill-ports-and-start.sh`
- **New:** `scripts/start-dev.sh` (and `start-dev.ps1` on Windows)

Usage:
```bash
./scripts/start-dev.sh        # Start backend + frontend
./scripts/start-dev.sh backend
./scripts/start-dev.sh frontend
```

---

## Database setup

- **Tables:** `auth_users`, `users`, `cameras`, `detections`, `objects`, `unknown_faces`, `alerts`
- **Migrations:** Applied successfully
- **Admin user:** `admin@visioryx.dev` / `admin123`

---

## Fixes applied

1. **Alert model:** Renamed `metadata` → `meta` (avoids SQLAlchemy reserved name)
2. **Auth:** Switched from passlib to `bcrypt` for hashing/verification
3. **Admin email:** `admin@visioryx.local` → `admin@visioryx.dev` (valid email format)
4. **Next.js:** Removed invalid WebSocket rewrite from `next.config.js`

---

## Services status

- **Backend:** http://localhost:8000 (running)
- **API docs:** http://localhost:8000/api/docs
- **Login:** `POST /api/v1/auth/login` with `admin@visioryx.dev` / `admin123`
- **Frontend:** Start with `cd frontend && npm run dev` (or `./scripts/start-dev.sh frontend`)

---

## Quick start

```bash
# Ensure Postgres is running on port 5432 (Docker or local)
# Then:
./scripts/start-dev.sh
```

Then open http://localhost:3000, log in with `admin@visioryx.dev` / `admin123`, and use the dashboard.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Handling activation script**

I'm investigating why the activation script fails on different platforms, as the shell script exits on error and may not handle missing venv gracefully. The activation script fails on macOS because venv/Scripts/activate doesn't exist, causing the whole line to return 1 and exit the script due to `set -e`. I need to check if venv exists before sourcing activate to avoid set -e triggering on failed activation. I'll add error handling around venv creation and activation to prevent set -e from triggering on failed activation. 

I'm making the activation script more robust by adding conditional checks for venv existence and using selective set -e usage. I'm adding `|| true` to the activation command to prevent exit code 1 when both activation paths fail. </details></think>

---


Fixing the start script and continuing with the next phases.

Summary of changes:

---

## 1. `start-dev.sh` updates

- Removed `set -e` so the script doesn’t exit on the first error
- Fixed venv activation for macOS and Windows
- Added `wait` for backend/frontend so the script keeps running until you stop it

---

## 2. New and updated dashboard pages

| Page | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Live Monitoring** (`/live`) | Lists cameras with stream placeholders |
| **Cameras** | Full CRUD: add, edit, delete cameras |
| **Users** | Register users with name and email |
| **Detections** | Table of detection history |
| **Overview** | Uses shared `api()` helper |

---

## 3. Shared API client

- `src/lib/api.ts` – `api()` and `getToken()` for authenticated requests

---

## 4. Auth behavior

- `AuthGuard` – Redirects to `/login` when not authenticated
- Logout button – Clears token and redirects to login

---

## How to run

```bash
./scripts/start-dev.sh        # Backend + frontend
./scripts/start-dev.sh backend   # Backend only (keeps running)
./scripts/start-dev.sh frontend  # Frontend only
```

Then open http://localhost:3000, log in with `admin@visioryx.dev` / `admin123`, and use the dashboard.

Summary of changes:

---

## Phase status

| Phase | Status |
|-------|--------|
| **1** | Project setup, DB, config |
| **2** | APIs, Auth, Face Recognition, Object Detection |
| **3** | Camera workers, WebSocket, Dashboard UI |
| **4** | Alerts, Analytics, Face upload, UI polish |

---

## Dependency updates

**Frontend** (`package.json`):
- Next.js 14.1 → 14.2.18
- React 18.2 → 18.3.1
- MUI 5.15 → 5.16.7
- Recharts 2.12 → 2.13.3
- date-fns 3.3 → 4.1
- zustand 4.5 → 5.0.1
- TypeScript, Tailwind, PostCSS, etc. updated

**Backend** (`requirements.txt`):
- FastAPI, uvicorn, SQLAlchemy, asyncpg, alembic
- OpenCV, numpy, insightface, ultralytics
- bcrypt, pydantic, python-dotenv
- Removed `asyncio-throttle` (unused)

---

## UI updates

- **Theme**: Primary blue (#0d47a1), rounded corners, card shadows
- **AppBar**: Gradient background
- **Login page**: Gradient background, updated layout
- **Home page**: Gradient hero with Login/Dashboard buttons
- **Dashboard**: Live/Offline chip for WebSocket status

---

## New features

1. **Face upload** – Users page: upload icon per user to add face images
2. **WebSocket** – `useWebSocket` hook; dashboard refreshes on detection events
3. **API helper** – Supports FormData for file uploads

---

## Apply changes

```bash
cd frontend && npm install
cd ../backend && pip install -r requirements.txt
./scripts/start-dev.sh
```

Then open http://localhost:3000 and log in with `admin@visioryx.dev` / `admin123`.

## 1. Duplicate `inter` in `layout.tsx`

Removed the extra `const inter` declaration and kept a single definition.

---

## 2. Start script path

`./scripts/start-dev.sh` must be run from the **project root** (Visioryx), not from `backend/`.

Added a `start.sh` script at the project root so you can run:

```bash
cd /Users/devendervutukuru/Desktop/My\ Projects/Visioryx
./start.sh
```

`start.sh` will change to the project root and then run `scripts/start-dev.sh`.

```bash
./start.sh
```

Or:

```bash
cd /Users/devendervutukuru/Desktop/My\ Projects/Visioryx
./scripts/start-dev.sh
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Adding test camera**

I'm adding a test camera with rtsp_url='0' for webcam testing. 

**Handling CORS**

The img src request to backend on port 8000 from frontend on port 3000 will trigger CORS, which may cause issues with webcam feed display. The frontend can access /api/v1/stream/1/mjpeg?token= via same-origin rewrite since /api/* routes are already configured to proxy to the backend. The img src will hit localhost:8000/api/v1/stream/1/mjpeg, triggering a CORS preflight since it's cross-origin, but the backend allows localhost:3000 origin. </details></think>

Phase 5 is done. Summary of what was added:

---

## Phase 5: Live Camera Streaming & Detection Logging

### 1. **Stream Manager** (`backend/app/services/stream_manager.py`)
- Per-camera frame capture in background threads
- Frame buffer for MJPEG streaming
- `start_stream()` / `stop_stream()` for each camera

### 2. **MJPEG Stream API** (`backend/app/api/stream.py`)
- `GET /api/v1/stream/{camera_id}/mjpeg?token=JWT` – MJPEG stream for `<img src>`
- `POST /api/v1/stream/{camera_id}/start` – start capture
- `POST /api/v1/stream/{camera_id}/stop` – stop capture
- Token auth for streams (browsers can’t send Bearer in `<img>`)

### 3. **Live Monitoring Page**
- **Start Stream** – starts capture and shows live feed
- **Stop Stream** – stops capture
- Live/Stopped status chip
- MJPEG display via `<img src>` with token

### 4. **Logging Service** (`backend/app/services/logging_service.py`)
- `log_detection()` – writes to DB and broadcasts over WebSocket
- `create_alert()` – creates alerts for unknown faces
- WebSocket events: `face_recognized`, `unknown_person_detected`, `alert`

---

## How to Use

1. Add a camera in **Cameras** (e.g. RTSP URL or `0` for webcam).
2. Open **Live Monitoring**.
3. Click **Start Stream** on a camera.
4. Live feed appears in the card.

---

## RTSP / Webcam

- **RTSP**: `rtsp://user:pass@ip:554/stream`
- **Webcam**: use `0` as the RTSP URL (OpenCV default camera)

_**Agent (model composer-1.5, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Testing CP Plus compatibility**

I'm checking if CP Plus cameras with RTSP support can integrate with Visioryx. I'm verifying CP Plus DVR RTSP integration with Visioryx using standard RTSP URL formats. </details></think>

---


Yes, it can work. CP Plus DVRs usually expose RTSP streams that Visioryx can use.

## RTSP URL format

Typical patterns for CP Plus DVRs:

```
rtsp://admin:avks-2605@192.168.0.3:554/stream1
rtsp://admin:avks-2605@192.168.0.3:554/stream2
rtsp://admin:avks-2605@192.168.0.3:554/channel1
rtsp://admin:avks-2605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0
```

Port `554` is the default RTSP port.

## Adding in Visioryx

1. Open **Cameras** in the dashboard.
2. Click **Add Camera**.
3. Use:
   - **Name**: e.g. `Office Cam 1`
   - **RTSP URL**: one of the URLs above (adjust channel/stream number as needed)

## Finding the correct URL

CP Plus DVRs vary by model. Try:

1. **KVMS Pro** – check its settings or documentation for the RTSP URL or stream path.
2. **DVR web UI** – log in at `http://192.168.0.3` and look for RTSP/stream settings.
3. **VLC** – open `Media → Open Network Stream` and test URLs like:
   - `rtsp://admin:avks-2605@192.168.0.3:554/stream1`
   - `rtsp://admin:avks-2605@192.168.0.3:554/`

## Security

Do not hardcode credentials in the codebase. Store them only in the RTSP URL when adding the camera in the dashboard, or use environment variables if you add a config option for credentials.

## Network

- Visioryx backend must be on the same network as the DVR (or have a route to `192.168.0.3`).
- If Visioryx runs in Docker, ensure it can reach `192.168.0.3`.

If you share the exact CP Plus DVR model or the RTSP URL shown in KVMS Pro, we can narrow down the correct URL format.

Here’s what’s going on and how to fix it.

---

## Password encoding

Your password is **`avks@2605`** (with `@`), not `avks-2605`.

In RTSP URLs, `@` separates credentials from the host, so it must be encoded as **`%40`**.

Use: **`avks%402605`** in the RTSP URL.

---

## RTSP URLs to try (in order)

### 1. Dahua/Hikvision style (most common for CP Plus)

```
rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0
```

For other channels, change `channel=1` to `channel=2`, `channel=3`, etc.

### 2. Stream path style

```
rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/stream1
rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/stream2
```

### 3. VideoInput style

```
rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/VideoInput/1/mpeg4/1
```

---

## Quick test with VLC

1. Open VLC → **Media** → **Open Network Stream** (Ctrl+N).
2. Paste: `rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0`
3. Click **Play**.

If it works in VLC, use the same URL in Visioryx.

---

## Add in Visioryx

1. **Cameras** → **Add Camera**
2. **Name:** Office Cam 1
3. **RTSP URL:** `rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0`
4. **Live Monitoring** → **Start Stream**

---

## Finding the exact path in KVMS Pro

1. Select device `192.168.0.3` in KVMS Pro.
2. Click **Link to WEB** to open the DVR web interface.
3. Check **Network** for RTSP port (often 554).
4. Check **Camera** for stream path or RTSP settings.

A setup guide with more formats is in `docs/CP-PLUS-RTSP-SETUP.md`.

Here’s what’s going on and how to fix it.

## Cause

The backend on port 8000 does **not** have the stream routes registered. The OpenAPI spec shows 17 routes and none of them are stream endpoints. The stream router is defined in `main.py`, so the running backend was likely started before the stream router was added.

## Fix

Restart the backend so it loads the stream router:

```bash
# Stop the current backend (Ctrl+C in its terminal), then:
./scripts/start-dev.sh
```

Or only the backend:

```bash
./scripts/start-dev.sh backend
```

## Changes made

1. **Startup log** – `main.py` now logs when the stream API is registered:
   ```
   Stream API registered: /api/v1/stream/{camera_id}/start, /stop, /mjpeg
   ```

2. **Troubleshooting section** – Added to `README.md` for this 404 case.

## After restart

1. Confirm the log line above appears when the backend starts.
2. Open Live Monitoring and click **Start Stream** on a camera.
3. The stream should start and the feed should appear.

If you still see 404 after restarting, share the backend startup logs.

---


You’re looking for **RTSP** settings (Real-Time Streaming Protocol). CP Plus DVRs and KVMS PRO usually **don’t expose a separate “RTSP settings” page**.

## What KVMS PRO shows

From your screenshots:

- **Device CFG** → Camera (Encode), Network, Event, Storage, System  
- **Network** → TCP/IP, Connect, PPPOE, DDNS, etc.  
- **Camera** → Encode (resolution, bitrate, etc.)

There is no dedicated RTSP/RTSL menu. That’s normal for many CP Plus devices.

## What to do instead

RTSP is usually enabled by default on port 554. You can use standard URLs without changing any RTSP-specific settings.

### 1. Use the web interface

1. In KVMS PRO, select device **192.168.0.3**
2. Click **Link to WEB**
3. Log in (admin / avks@2605)
4. Check **Network** or **Camera** for any RTSP/stream options (some models show them there)

### 2. Try standard RTSP URLs

Your project already has a CP Plus RTSP guide. Use these URLs directly:

```
rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0
rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=2&subtype=0
```

Important: use `avks%402605` (not `avks@2605`) because of the `@` in the password.

### 3. Test in VLC

1. Open VLC → Media → Open Network Stream  
2. Paste: `rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0`  
3. If it plays, use the same URL in Visioryx.

### 4. Add in Visioryx

1. Go to **Cameras** → Add Camera  
2. **Camera Name:** e.g. Office Cam 1  
3. **RTSP URL:** `rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0`  
4. Save, then go to **Live Monitoring** → Start Stream  

---

**Summary:** KVMS PRO doesn’t show RTSP settings for your CP Plus DVR. Use the standard RTSP URLs above; they usually work without extra configuration. If you want, I can update the CP Plus RTSP doc to mention that KVMS PRO has no RTSP menu.

```json
          },
          {

          },

          },

          },

          },
          {

          {

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },

          },
          {
```

```json
      "codeBlock": {

        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx - Camera Stream Manager",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "Manages per-camera frame capture and MJPEG streaming.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "import asyncio",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "import threading",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "import cv2",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "import numpy as np",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.config import get_settings",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.logger import get_logger",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "logger = get_logger(\"stream_manager\")",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "# Global: camera_id -&gt; latest JPEG bytes",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "_frame_buffer: dict[int, bytes] = {}",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "_frame_lock = threading.Lock()",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "_active_cameras: dict[int, dict] = {}  # camera_id -&gt; {rtsp_url, thread, stop_event}",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "def _capture_loop(camera_id: int, rtsp_url: str, stop_event: threading.Event):",
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Background thread: capture frames and update buffer.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    cap = cv2.VideoCapture(rtsp_url)",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "    if not cap.isOpened():",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "        logger.error(f\"Camera {camera_id}: failed to open {rtsp_url}\")",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "        return",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    settings = get_settings()",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    skip = settings.FRAME_SKIP_RATE",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "    count = 0",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    try:",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "        while not stop_event.is_set():",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "            ret, frame = cap.read()",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "            if not ret or frame is None:",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "                break",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "            count += 1",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "            if count % (skip + 1) == 0:",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "                _, jpeg = cv2.imencode(\".jpg\", frame)",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "                with _frame_lock:",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "                    _frame_buffer[camera_id] = jpeg.tobytes()",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    except Exception as e:",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "        logger.error(f\"Camera {camera_id} capture error: {e}\")",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    finally:",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "        cap.release()",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "        with _frame_lock:",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "            _frame_buffer.pop(camera_id, None)",
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "def start_stream(camera_id: int, rtsp_url: str) -&gt; bool:",
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Start capturing from camera.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 51
          }
        ]
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Updating camera status**

The camera status is never persisted to the database, only broadcast via WebSocket. I need to persist the AI detection pipeline status to the database since camera workers only broadcast via WebSocket and don't update the DB. The camera status isn't persisting to the DB because the AI pipeline status updates happen via WebSocket, not database writes, and the Live Monitoring uses a different system entirely. </details></think>

---

Status is now updated correctly. Here’s what was going on:

## Why it showed "inactive"

1. **Default value** – New cameras are stored with `status = "inactive"` in the database.
2. **No updates** – When you clicked "Start Stream" on Live Monitoring, the stream started but the database status was never changed.
3. **Cameras page** – It reads status from the API (database), so it always showed "inactive".

## What was changed

The stream API now updates the camera status in the database when you start or stop a stream:

- **Start stream** → `status` set to `"active"`
- **Stop stream** → `status` set to `"inactive"`

## What you’ll see

1. Go to **Live Monitoring** and click **Start Stream** for the AVKS camera.
2. Go back to **Cameras** (or refresh the page).
3. The status should show **active** (green chip) while the stream is running.
4. Click **Stop Stream** on Live Monitoring, then refresh Cameras; the status should return to **inactive**.

Restart the backend so these changes take effect.

For example, `%` should be encoded as `%25`.[2]<br/>- **Credentials verification**: Even if credentials appear correct, double-check them against what works in VLC.[1]<br/>- **Alternative approach**: If `cv2.VideoCapture()` continues to fail with special characters, the `imutils` library's `VideoStream` can be used as a workaround.[2]<br/><br/>## rtsp_transport tcp Configuration<br/><br/>The "Nonmatching transport in server reply" error often requires specifying TCP transport:[3][4]<br/><br/>- **Environment variable**: Set `OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS` to change the transport protocol. For OpenCV 3.4.1+, use:[3]<br/>  ```<br/>  export OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS="rtsp_transport;tcp"<br/>  ```<br/>  or in Python:<br/>  ```<br/>  _putenv_s("OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS", "rtsp_transport;tcp")<br/>  ```<br/><br/>- **FFmpeg command line equivalent**: The setting corresponds to ffplay's `-rtsp_transport tcp` parameter.[3][4]<br/><br/>- **Note**: This option is hardcoded in OpenCV's ffmpeg implementation, so the environment variable is necessary to override the default UDP transport.[3] |

</details>

[Timeout when grabbing frame from RTSP stream · Issue #27091 · opencv/opencv](https://github.com/opencv/opencv/issues/27091)<br/><br/># Setting OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS for RTSP TCP Transport in Python<br/><br/>To use TCP protocol for RTSP streams in OpenCV with Python, you need to set the `OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS` environment variable before opening the video capture.[4]<br/><br/>## Basic Usage<br/><br/>```python<br/>import os<br/>import cv2<br/><br/>os.environ["OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS"] = "rtsp_transport;tcp"<br/>cap = cv2.VideoCapture("rtsp://your_ip:port/stream", cv2.CAP_FFMPEG)<br/>```<br/><br/>## Alternative: UDP Transport<br/><br/>If your RTSP source only supports UDP, use:[4]<br/><br/>```python<br/>os.environ["OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS"] = "rtsp_transport;udp"<br/>```<br/><br/>## Important Notes<br/><br/>- By default, OpenCV's FFMPEG backend uses TCP for RTSP connections.[1] If your stream only supports UDP, you must set the environment variable to override this.<br/>- The `OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS` variable must be set **before** calling `cv2.VideoCapture()`.[4]<br/>- Ensure your OpenCV installation includes FFMPEG support. You can verify this by running: `python -c "import cv2; print(cv2.getBuildInformation())"` and checking for FFMPEG support.[4]<br/><br/>## Modern Approach (OpenCV 4.6+)<br/><br/>OpenCV 4.6.0 and later default to `rtsp_flags=prefer_tcp`, which automatically attempts TCP first and falls back to UDP if needed, eliminating the need to manually set transport options in most cases.[1] |

Here’s what’s going on and what was changed.

## What the logs show

1. **`[rtsp @ ...] method OPTIONS failed: 401 Unauthorized`** – The DVR is rejecting the RTSP credentials.
2. **`OpenCV: Couldn't read video stream`** – OpenCV can’t open the stream because of the auth failure.

So the black screen is caused by the RTSP connection failing due to authentication.

## Changes made

1. **RTSP transport** – The stream manager now uses TCP for RTSP (`rtsp_transport;tcp`), which is more reliable than UDP.
2. **FFmpeg backend** – OpenCV is explicitly using the FFmpeg backend for RTSP.
3. **Docs** – Added troubleshooting for 401 and black screen in `docs/CP-PLUS-RTSP-SETUP.md`.

## What to do next

### 1. Verify credentials

The DVR is returning 401, so the username/password in the RTSP URL are likely wrong.

- If the DVR web login uses **`avks@2605`**, the RTSP URL must use `avks%402605` (because `@` must be encoded in URLs):

  ```
  rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0
  ```

- If the correct password is **`admin-2605`**, keep using that.

### 2. Test in VLC

1. Open VLC → Media → Open Network Stream.
2. Paste the RTSP URL.
3. If it plays in VLC, use the same URL in Visioryx.
4. If it fails in VLC, fix the URL/credentials first.

### 3. Update the camera in Visioryx

1. Go to **Cameras** → Edit the AVKS camera.
2. Set the RTSP URL to the one that works in VLC.
3. Restart the backend, then try **Live Monitoring** again.

Restart the backend so the RTSP transport changes take effect. The main fix is using the correct DVR credentials in the RTSP URL.

Using the DVR's IP address: 192.168.0.3 (the address in your browser).

RTSP URL format:
```
rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0
```

- Username: admin
- Password: avks@2605 → use avks%402605 in the URL (encode @ as %40)
- IP: 192.168.0.3
- Port: 554
- Path: /cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0

Update the camera in Visioryx:

1. Go to **Cameras** → Edit the AVKS camera.
2. Set **RTSP URL** to:
   ```
   rtsp://admin:avks%402605@192.168.0.3:554/cam/realmonitor?channel=1&subtype=0
   ```
3. Save, then open **Live Monitoring** and click **Start Stream**.

The 401 error was likely caused by using `admin-2605` instead of `avks%402605`. With the correct password, the stream should work.

```json
            "text": "async def _generate_mjpeg(camera_id: int):",

            "text": "    \"\"\"Yield MJPEG frames for streaming.\"\"\"",

          },

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 37
          },

            "lineNumber": 39
          },

          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 43

          {
            "text": "def _detect_faces_opencv(frame: np.ndarray) -&gt; list[dict]:",
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Fallback: OpenCV Haar cascade (no embedding).\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "    gray = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "    cascade = cv2.CascadeClassifier(",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "        cv2.data.haarcascades + \"haarcascade_frontalface_default.xml\"",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "    )",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "    rects = cascade.detectMultiScale(gray, 1.3, 5)",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "    return [",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "        {",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"bbox\": [int(x), int(y), int(x + w), int(y + h)],",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"landmarks\": None,",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"embedding\": None,",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"det_score\": 1.0,",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "        for (x, y, w, h) in rects",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "    ]",
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.31723475456237793

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./backend/app/ai/object_detector.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 29,

          }

        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "def detect_objects(frame: np.ndarray) -&gt; list[dict]:",
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    Detect objects in BGR frame.",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns list of {name, confidence, bbox: [x1,y1,x2,y2]}",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "    model = _get_model()",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "    if model == \"unavailable\":",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "        return []",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "    settings = get_settings()",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    results = model(frame, conf=settings.OBJECT_DETECTION_CONFIDENCE, verbose=False)",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "    detections = []",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    for r in results:",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "        if r.boxes is None:",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "            continue",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "        names = r.names or {}",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "        for box in r.boxes:",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "            cls_id = int(box.cls[0])",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "            name = names.get(cls_id, \"unknown\")",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "            if name not in TARGET_CLASSES:",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "                continue  # Filter to target classes",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "            xyxy = box.xyxy[0].tolist()",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "            conf = float(box.conf[0])",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "            detections.append({",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"object_name\": name,",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"confidence\": conf,",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"bbox\": [int(x) for x in xyxy],",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "            })",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "    return detections",
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.30475690960884094

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "from app.api import stream",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "app.include_router(stream.router, prefix=f\"{settings.API_V1_PREFIX}/stream\", tags=[\"stream\"])",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "logger.info(\"Stream API registered: /api/v1/stream/{camera_id}/start, /stop, /mjpeg\")",

          },

          {
            "text": "async def websocket_endpoint(websocket: WebSocket):",

          {

          {

          },
          {
            "text": "    client_id = str(uuid.uuid4())",

          },
          {
            "text": "    await ws_manager.connect(websocket, client_id)",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "    try:",

          },
          {
            "text": "        while True:",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "            data = await websocket.receive_text()",

          },
          {
            "text": "            # Heartbeat / keepalive",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "            if data == \"ping\":",

          },
          {
            "text": "                await websocket.send_text(\"pong\")",

          },
          {
            "text": "    except Exception:",

          },

            "text": "        pass",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "    finally:",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "        ws_manager.disconnect(client_id)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 58
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Visioryx",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "**AI Powered Real-Time Face Recognition &amp; Surveillance System**",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "Developed by: Devender Vutukuru",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "## Overview",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx is a production-grade real-time AI surveillance system with:",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Face Recognition** — Known person identification",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Unknown Face Detection** — Detection and clustering of unregistered individuals",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Object Detection** — YOLOv8 for person, phone, laptop, bag, etc.",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Multi-Camera Monitoring** — Per-camera workers with async pipelines",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Real-Time Alerts** — WebSocket push to dashboard",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Analytics Dashboard** — Detection trends, charts, searchable history",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "**100% open-source. No paid APIs.**",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "## Technology Stack",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "| Layer | Technologies |",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "|-------|-------------|",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "| Backend | Python, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, OpenCV, NumPy |",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "| Face Recognition | InsightFace (or face_recognition) |",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "| Object Detection | YOLOv8 (Ultralytics) |",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "| Frontend | Next.js 14, React, TypeScript, TailwindCSS |",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "| Dashboard UI | [material-kit-react](https://github.com/minimal-ui-kit/material-kit-react) (MIT, optional reference) |",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "| Database | PostgreSQL |",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "| Real-time | WebSockets |",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "| Deployment | Docker, Docker Compose |",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "## Project Structure",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "ai-surveillance-system/",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "├── backend/",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "│   ├── app/",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "│   │   ├── core/         # config, security, websocket, logger",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },

            "text": "\"\"\"",

      "codeBlock": {

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "#!/usr/bin/env python3",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx - Face Training Script",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "Batch extract face embeddings for users with image_path but no embedding.",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "Usage: python scripts/train_faces.py",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "import asyncio",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "import sys",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "from pathlib import Path",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \"backend\"))",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "from sqlalchemy import select",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, AsyncSession",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.config import get_settings",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.database.models import User",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.ai.face_embedding import extract_embeddings_from_image",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "async def train():",
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    settings = get_settings()",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "    engine = create_async_engine(settings.DATABASE_URL)",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    async_session = sessionmaker(engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "    async with async_session() as db:",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "        result = await db.execute(select(User).where(User.image_path.isnot(None)))",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "        users = result.scalars().all()",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "        updated = 0",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "        for user in users:",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "            if user.face_embedding:",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "                continue",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "            if not user.image_path or not os.path.exists(user.image_path):",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "                print(f\"Skip {user.email}: image not found\")",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "                continue",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "            embeddings = extract_embeddings_from_image(user.image_path)",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "            if not embeddings:",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "                print(f\"Skip {user.email}: no face detected\")",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "                continue",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "            user.face_embedding = embeddings[0]",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "            updated += 1",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "            print(f\"Updated: {user.email}\")",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "        await db.commit()",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(f\"Updated {updated} users\")",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "    await engine.dispose()",
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "if __name__ == \"__main__\":",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    import os",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "    asyncio.run(train())",
            "lineNumber": 52
          }
        ]
      },

          },
          {
```

```json
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx - Core Configuration",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "Centralized configuration management using Pydantic Settings.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "from functools import lru_cache",

          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },

            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "class Settings(BaseSettings):",

          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Application settings loaded from environment variables.\"\"\"",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(",

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "        env_file_encoding=\"utf-8\",",

          },
          {
            "text": "        case_sensitive=False,",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },

            "text": "    )",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Application",

          },

            "text": "    APP_NAME: str = \"Visioryx\"",

          },
          {
            "text": "    APP_VERSION: str = \"1.0.0\"",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "    # API",

          },
          {
            "text": "    API_V1_PREFIX: str = \"/api/v1\"",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Database",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    DATABASE_URL: str = \"postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx\"",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    # JWT Security",

          },

          },
          {

          },

          },
          {

          },

          },

          },

          },
          {

          },

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx - Recognition Pipeline",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "Process frame: detect faces, match, classify known/unknown.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "import cv2",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "import numpy as np",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.ai.face_detector import detect_faces",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.ai.face_matcher import find_best_match",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.config import get_settings",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.logger import get_logger",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "logger = get_logger(\"recognition_pipeline\")",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "async def process_frame(",
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    frame: np.ndarray,",
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    db_embeddings: list[tuple[int, list[float]]],",
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; list[dict]:",
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    Process single frame for face recognition.",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns list of {user_id?, status, confidence, bbox, embedding}",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "    settings = get_settings()",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "    faces = detect_faces(frame)",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    results = []",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    for f in faces:",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "        bbox = f[\"bbox\"]",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "        embedding = f.get(\"embedding\")",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "        det_score = f.get(\"det_score\", 1.0)",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "        if det_score &lt; settings.FACE_DETECTION_CONFIDENCE:",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "            continue",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "        if embedding is None:",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "            results.append({",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"status\": \"unknown\",",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"confidence\": 0,",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"bbox\": bbox,",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"user_id\": None,",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "            })",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "            continue",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "        match = find_best_match(embedding, db_embeddings)",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "        if match:",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "            user_id, sim = match",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "            results.append({",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"user_id\": user_id,",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"status\": \"known\",",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"confidence\": sim,",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"bbox\": bbox,",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"embedding\": embedding,",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "            })",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "        else:",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "            results.append({",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"user_id\": None,",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"status\": \"unknown\",",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"confidence\": 0,",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"bbox\": bbox,",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"embedding\": embedding,",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "            })",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "    return results",
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.3647094964981079

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 166
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 248
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Run migrations (first time)",
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "text": "docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml exec backend alembic upgrade head",
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "# Seed admin (first time)",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml exec backend python scripts/seed_admin.py",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "text": "## Environment Variables",
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "text": "### Backend (&#96;.env&#96;)",
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180
          },
          {
            "text": "| Variable | Description | Default |",
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "|----------|-------------|---------|",
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;DATABASE_URL&#96; | Async PostgreSQL URL | &#96;postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx&#96; |",
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;DATABASE_URL_SYNC&#96; | Sync URL for Alembic | &#96;postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx&#96; |",
            "lineNumber": 184
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;SECRET_KEY&#96; | JWT secret (use &#96;openssl rand -hex 32&#96;) | — |",
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;FACE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD&#96; | Cosine similarity for known | &#96;0.6&#96; |",
            "lineNumber": 186
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;DEBUG&#96; | Enable debug mode | &#96;false&#96; |",
            "lineNumber": 187
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188
          },
          {
            "text": "### Frontend (&#96;.env.local&#96;)",
            "lineNumber": 189
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190
          },
          {
            "text": "| Variable | Description |",
            "lineNumber": 191
          },
          {
            "text": "|----------|-------------|",
            "lineNumber": 192
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL&#96; | Backend API URL |",
            "lineNumber": 193
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL&#96; | WebSocket URL |",
            "lineNumber": 194
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 196
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197
          },
          {
            "text": "## Database Migrations",
            "lineNumber": 198
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 200
          },
          {
            "text": "cd backend",
            "lineNumber": 201
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202
          },
          {
            "text": "# Create new migration",
            "lineNumber": 203
          },
          {
            "text": "alembic revision -m \"description\"",
            "lineNumber": 204
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "text": "# Apply migrations",
            "lineNumber": 206
          },
          {
            "text": "alembic upgrade head",
            "lineNumber": 207
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 208
          },
          {
            "text": "# Rollback",
            "lineNumber": 209
          },
          {
            "text": "alembic downgrade -1",
            "lineNumber": 210
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 211
          },

            "lineNumber": 212
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 213
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 214
          },
          {
            "text": "## Scripts",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 216
          },
          {
            "text": "| Script | Purpose |",
            "lineNumber": 217
          },
          {
            "text": "|--------|---------|",
            "lineNumber": 218
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;scripts/start-dev.sh&#96; | Start dev (kills 3000/8000 if busy) |",
            "lineNumber": 219
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;scripts/register_user.py&#96; | Register user with face image |",
            "lineNumber": 220
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;scripts/train_faces.py&#96; | Batch extract embeddings for users |",
            "lineNumber": 221
          },
          {
            "text": "| &#96;backend/scripts/seed_admin.py&#96; | Create admin@visioryx.dev / admin123 |",
            "lineNumber": 222
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 223
          },
          {
            "text": "## Phase Status",
            "lineNumber": 224
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 225
          },
          {
            "text": "| Phase | Status | Description |",
            "lineNumber": 226
          },
          {
            "text": "|-------|--------|-------------|",
            "lineNumber": 227
          },
          {
            "text": "| 1 | ✅ | Project setup, DB, config |",
            "lineNumber": 228
          },
          {
            "text": "| 2 | ✅ | APIs, Auth, Face Recognition, Object Detection |",
            "lineNumber": 229
          },
          {
            "text": "| 3 | ✅ | Camera workers, WebSocket, Dashboard UI |",
            "lineNumber": 230
          },
          {
            "text": "| 4 | ✅ | Alerts, Analytics, Face upload, UI polish |",
            "lineNumber": 231
          },
          {
            "text": "| 5 | ✅ | Live MJPEG streaming, Stream manager, Detection logging |",
            "lineNumber": 232
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 233
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 234
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 235
          },
          {
            "text": "## Troubleshooting",
            "lineNumber": 236
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 237
          },
          {
            "text": "### Stream returns 404 (POST /api/v1/stream/1/start)",
            "lineNumber": 238
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 239
          },
          {
            "text": "The stream routes are registered when the backend starts. If you see 404, **restart the backend** so it picks up the stream router:",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 241
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 242
          },
          {
            "text": "# Stop any running backend (Ctrl+C or kill port 8000), then:",

          },
          {
            "text": "./scripts/start-dev.sh backend",
            "lineNumber": 244
          },
          {
            "text": "# Or full stack:",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 246
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",

          }
        ]

            "text": "\"\"\"",

          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",

          },

            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "logger = get_logger(\"face_detector\")",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "# Lazy load to avoid import errors if not installed",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "_face_app = None",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "def _get_face_app():",

          {

          },

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 24
          },

            "text": "            _face_app = FaceAnalysis(name=\"buffalo_l\", root=\"models/insightface\")",

          },
          {
            "text": "            _face_app.prepare(ctx_id=0, det_size=(640, 640))",

          },

          },

          },

          },
          {

          },

          },

          },

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./backend/app/ai/face_matcher.py",
        "range": {

        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "def find_best_match(",
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    embedding: list[float],",
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    db_embeddings: list[tuple[int, list[float]]],",
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; Optional[tuple[int, float]]:",
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    Find best matching user by cosine similarity.",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns (user_id, similarity) or None if below threshold.",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "    settings = get_settings()",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    threshold = settings.FACE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "    best_id = None",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "    best_sim = -1.0",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    for user_id, db_emb in db_embeddings:",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "        if db_emb is None or len(db_emb) == 0:",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "            continue",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "        sim = cosine_similarity(embedding, db_emb)",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "        if sim &gt; best_sim:",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "            best_sim = sim",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "            best_id = user_id",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    if best_id is not None and best_sim &gt;= threshold:",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "        return (best_id, best_sim)",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    return None",
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.3077491521835327

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
```
