          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Visioryx",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "**AI Powered Real-Time Face Recognition &amp; Surveillance System**",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "Developed by: Devender Vutukuru",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "## Overview",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx is a production-grade real-time AI surveillance system with:",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Face Recognition** — Known person identification",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Unknown Face Detection** — Detection and clustering of unregistered individuals",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Object Detection** — YOLOv8 for person, phone, laptop, bag, etc.",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Multi-Camera Monitoring** — Per-camera workers with async pipelines",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Real-Time Alerts** — WebSocket push to dashboard",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Analytics Dashboard** — Detection trends, charts, searchable history",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "**100% open-source. No paid APIs.**",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "## Technology Stack",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "| Layer | Technologies |",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "|-------|-------------|",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "| Backend | Python, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, OpenCV, NumPy |",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "| Face Recognition | InsightFace (or face_recognition) |",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "| Object Detection | YOLOv8 (Ultralytics) |",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "| Frontend | Next.js 14, React, TypeScript, TailwindCSS |",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "| Dashboard UI | [material-kit-react](https://github.com/minimal-ui-kit/material-kit-react) (MIT, optional reference) |",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "| Database | PostgreSQL |",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "| Real-time | WebSockets |",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "| Deployment | Docker, Docker Compose |",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "## Project Structure",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "ai-surveillance-system/",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "├── backend/",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "│   ├── app/",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "│   │   ├── api/          # auth, users, cameras, detections, analytics",

          },
          {
            "text": "│   │   ├── core/         # config, security, websocket, logger",

          },

          },

          {
            "text": "def detect_faces(frame: np.ndarray) -&gt; list[dict]:",
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "    Detect faces in BGR frame.",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns list of {bbox: [x1,y1,x2,y2], landmarks, embedding (if available)}",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "    app = _get_face_app()",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "    if app == \"opencv\":",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "        return _detect_faces_opencv(frame)",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    faces = app.get(frame)",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "    result = []",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    for f in faces:",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "        emb = None",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "        if hasattr(f, \"embedding\") and f.embedding is not None:",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "            emb = f.embedding.tolist()",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "        result.append({",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"bbox\": f.bbox.astype(int).tolist(),",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"landmarks\": getattr(f, \"kps\", None),",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"embedding\": emb,",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"det_score\": float(getattr(f, \"det_score\", 1.0)),",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "        })",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "    return result",
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "def _detect_faces_opencv(frame: np.ndarray) -&gt; list[dict]:",
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Fallback: OpenCV Haar cascade (no embedding).\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 58
          }
        ]

          },
          {
            "text": ".\\scripts\\start-dev.ps1 -Target all",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "## Phase 1 — Installation",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "### Prerequisites",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "- Python 3.10+",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "- Node.js 20+",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "- PostgreSQL 14+",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "- Docker &amp; Docker Compose (optional)",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "### 1. Backend Setup",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "cd backend",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "text": "# Create virtual environment",
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "python -m venv venv",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "source venv/bin/activate   # Windows: venv\\Scripts\\activate",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "# Install dependencies",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "pip install -r requirements.txt",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "# Copy environment",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "cp .env.example .env",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "# Edit .env with your DATABASE_URL and SECRET_KEY",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "# Run migrations",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "alembic upgrade head",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "# Start API server",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000",
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": "### 2. Database (PostgreSQL)",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "text": "Create database:",
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;sql",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "text": "CREATE DATABASE visioryx;",
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "Or use Docker:",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "text": "docker run -d --name visioryx-db \\",
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "text": "  -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres \\",
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "text": "  -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \\",
            "lineNumber": 128
          },
          {
            "text": "  -e POSTGRES_DB=visioryx \\",
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "text": "  -p 5432:5432 \\",
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "text": "  postgres:16-alpine",
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133
          },
          {
            "text": "### 3. Frontend Setup",
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "text": "cd frontend",
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138
          },
          {
            "text": "# Install dependencies",
            "lineNumber": 139
          },
          {
            "text": "npm install",
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141
          },
          {
            "text": "# Copy environment",
            "lineNumber": 142
          },
          {
            "text": "cp .env.example .env.local",
            "lineNumber": 143
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144
          },
          {
            "text": "# Start dev server",
            "lineNumber": 145
          },
          {
            "text": "npm run dev",
            "lineNumber": 146
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 147
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "text": "Dashboard: http://localhost:3000  ",
            "lineNumber": 149
          },
          {
            "text": "API docs: http://localhost:8000/api/docs",
            "lineNumber": 150
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151
          },
          {
            "text": "### 4. Seed Admin User (First Time)",
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 154
          },
          {
            "text": "cd backend",
            "lineNumber": 155
          },
          {
            "text": "python scripts/seed_admin.py",
            "lineNumber": 156
          },
          {
            "text": "# Creates: admin@visioryx.dev / admin123",
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159
          },
          {
            "text": "### 5. Docker (Full Stack)",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "text": "# From project root",
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "cp backend/.env.example backend/.env",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "text": "docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up -d",
            "lineNumber": 166
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "text": "# Run migrations (first time)",
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "text": "docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml exec backend alembic upgrade head",
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "# Seed admin (first time)",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml exec backend python scripts/seed_admin.py",
            "lineNumber": 172
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.29122960567474365
    },

          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx - Face Embedding",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "Extract face embeddings for matching.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "def extract_embedding(frame: np.ndarray, bbox: Optional[list[int]] = None) -&gt; Optional[list[float]]:",
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "    Extract embedding for a single face.",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "    If bbox provided, crop and use; else use first detected face.",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    faces = detect_faces(frame)",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "    if not faces:",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "        return None",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    if bbox:",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "        for f in faces:",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "            if f.get(\"embedding\") is not None:",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "                return f[\"embedding\"]",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "        # No embedding from detector - would need separate model",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "        return None",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "    face = faces[0]",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    return face.get(\"embedding\")",
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "def extract_embeddings_from_image(image_path: str) -&gt; list[list[float]]:",
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Extract all face embeddings from image file.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 32
          }
        ]

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3
          },

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docker/Dockerfile.backend",
        "range": {

        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Visioryx - Backend Dockerfile",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "FROM python:3.11-slim",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "WORKDIR /app",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "# System dependencies for OpenCV and AI libraries",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "RUN apt-get update &amp;&amp; apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \\",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "    libgl1-mesa-glx \\",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "    libglib2.0-0 \\",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "    libsm6 \\",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "    libxext6 \\",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "    libxrender-dev \\",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "    libgomp1 \\",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "    &amp;&amp; rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "# Python dependencies",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "COPY backend/requirements.txt .",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "# Application",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "COPY backend/ .",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "COPY scripts/ ./scripts/",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "# Create storage directories",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "RUN mkdir -p storage/registered_faces storage/unknown_faces storage/snapshots models",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "ENV PYTHONPATH=/app",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "EXPOSE 8000",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "CMD [\"uvicorn\", \"app.main:app\", \"--host\", \"0.0.0.0\", \"--port\", \"8000\"]",
            "lineNumber": 32
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.27239990234375

          },
          {

          {

          },
          {
            "text": "# AI Powered Real-Time Face Recognition &amp; Surveillance System",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },

          },

            "text": "version: \"3.8\"",

          },

          },

          {

            "text": "    healthcheck:",

I'm clarifying the login flow by removing the Dashboard button from the home page to prevent bypassing authentication. 

**Loading embeddings**

I'm implementing a sync wrapper for the async recognition pipeline using nest_asyncio to handle nested event loops. </details></think>

Summary of changes:

## Changes made

### 1. **Face & object detection on live stream**
- Added `detection_overlay.py` to run face and object detection on live frames.
- Live streams now show:
  - **Green boxes** for recognized faces (known users)
  - **Orange boxes** for unknown faces
  - **Blue boxes** for objects (person, phone, laptop, etc.)
- Detection runs every 3rd frame to limit CPU use.

### 2. **User registration**
- **Users** page: **Register User** → add name and email → **Upload face** (cloud icon) for each user.
- Face embeddings are extracted automatically on upload (no need to run `train_faces.py`).
- Flow: Register User → Upload face photo → Face is used for recognition in Live Monitoring.

### 3. **Full screen and zoom**
- **Fullscreen** button on each live stream.
- **Zoom in / Zoom out** buttons (50%–200%).
- **Close** button to exit fullscreen.

### 4. **Login and dashboard access**
- Removed the **Dashboard** button from the landing page.
- Single **Sign In to Access Dashboard** button.
- Dashboard routes require login; unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login`.

### 5. **User registration flow**
- Added instructions on the Users page for registering users and uploading face photos.

---

## Face detection requirements

1. **Register users** – Users → Register User → add name and email.
2. **Upload face photos** – Click the upload icon for each user and add a clear frontal face photo.
3. **InsightFace** – Ensure `insightface` is installed (`pip install insightface`).
4. **Restart backend** – Restart the backend so the new detection overlay is loaded.

---

## Quick test

1. Restart backend: `./scripts/start-dev.sh`
2. Go to **Users** → Register a user → Upload a face photo.
3. Go to **Live Monitoring** → Start Stream on a camera.
4. You should see face boxes (green for known, orange for unknown) and object boxes on the stream.
5. Use the fullscreen and zoom controls on each stream.

---

Phase 1 — Project Setup & Architecture
Goal

Create the base project structure and install dependencies.

ai-surveillance-system
backend
frontend
models
storage

docker

Phase 2 — Database Setup
Goal

Create database models and connection.

Use PostgreSQL.

backend/app/database/models.py

Tables:

Run migration script.

Phase 3 — Face Detection System
Goal

Detect faces from camera feed.

Create:

backend/app/ai/face_detector.py

Use:

InsightFace

or OpenCV

Steps:

Capture frame

Detect faces

Return bounding boxes

Example output:

[
 {x:120,y:50,w:90,h:90}
]
Phase 4 — Face Embedding System
Goal

Convert face into numerical vector.

Create:

backend/app/ai/face_embedding.py

Each face becomes:

512 dimension vector

Example:

[0.223,0.193,0.442,...]

Store embeddings in database.

Phase 5 — Face Recognition Engine
Goal

Match detected faces with database users.

Create:

backend/app/ai/face_matcher.py

Matching method:

Cosine similarity

Logic:

similarity > threshold → known user
else → unknown

Threshold example:

0.6 – 0.7

Output:

{
name: "Devender",
confidence: 0.92
}
Phase 6 — Unknown Face System
Goal

Store unknown faces.

Create:

storage/unknown_faces

Process:

Save snapshot

Save embedding

Assign cluster id

Create:

backend/app/ai/unknown_clustering.py

Use clustering algorithm:

DBSCAN

Purpose:

Detect repeated unknown people.

Phase 7 — Object Detection
Goal

Detect objects in camera feed.

Use:

YOLOv8

Create:

backend/app/ai/object_detector.py

Detect objects:

person
phone
laptop
bag
bottle
chair
car
bike

Return:

[
 {object:"phone",confidence:0.87}
]
Phase 8 — Recognition Pipeline
Goal

Combine face + object detection.

Create:

backend/app/services/recognition_pipeline.py

Pipeline:

Frame
 ↓
Face Detection
 ↓
Face Embedding
 ↓
Face Matching
 ↓
Object Detection
 ↓
Bounding Box Rendering
 ↓
Event Logging

Boxes:

Green = known user
Red = unknown

Phase 9 — Camera Worker System
Goal

Support multiple cameras.

Create:

backend/app/services/camera_worker.py

Features:

Each camera runs in separate thread.

Sources:

Webcam
RTSP camera
IP camera
Video file

Example:

rtsp://camera-ip/stream
Phase 10 — Logging System
Goal

Store detection events.

Create:

backend/app/services/logging_service.py

Log:

user detected
unknown person
object detected
camera status

Snapshots stored in:

storage/snapshots
Phase 11 — WebSocket Live Updates
Goal

Real-time dashboard updates.

Create:

backend/app/core/websocket_manager.py

Events:

face_recognized
unknown_person
object_detected
camera_status

Frontend receives updates instantly.

Create script:

scripts/register_user.py

Process:

Save to database

Dashboard grid:

Camera 1

Each camera stream processed independently.

Here’s how the project compares to your phase plan:

---

# Visioryx vs Phase Plan – Status

## ✅ Implemented (Phases 1–12)

| Phase | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|------|
| **1 – Project Setup** | ✅ | Structure, venv, deps, config |
| **2 – Database** | ✅ | PostgreSQL, models (Users, Cameras, Detections, Objects, UnknownFace, Alerts, AuthUser), migrations |
| **3 – Face Detection** | ✅ | `face_detector.py` (InsightFace + OpenCV fallback) |
| **4 – Face Embedding** | ✅ | `face_embedding.py` (512-dim vectors) |
| **5 – Face Recognition** | ✅ | `face_matcher.py` (cosine similarity, threshold 0.6) |
| **6 – Unknown Face System** | ⚠️ Partial | `UnknownFace` model + API exist; clustering (`unknown_clustering.py`) exists but **not used** in the pipeline |
| **7 – Object Detection** | ✅ | `object_detector.py` (YOLOv8) |
| **8 – Recognition Pipeline** | ✅ | `recognition_pipeline.py` + `detection_overlay.py` (face + object boxes on stream) |
| **9 – Camera Worker** | ✅ | `camera_worker.py` + `stream_manager.py` (RTSP, MJPEG) |
| **10 – Logging System** | ⚠️ Partial | `logging_service.py` has `log_detection()` but **not called** from the stream pipeline |
| **11 – WebSocket** | ⚠️ Partial | WebSocket manager exists; `face_recognized` / `unknown_person_detected` not emitted from stream (because `log_detection` is not wired) |
| **12 – Admin Dashboard** | ✅ | Overview, Live, Users, Cameras, Detections, Analytics, Alerts |

## ✅ Implemented (Phases 13–17)

| Phase | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|------|
| **13 – User Registration** | ✅ | `register_user.py` + Users page (Register + Upload face, auto-embedding) |
| **14 – Multi-Camera** | ✅ | Live Monitoring grid, per-camera streams |
| **15 – Alerts** | ✅ | Alerts API + page; alerts created in `logging_service` |
| **16 – Performance** | ✅ | Frame skipping, async pipeline |
| **17 – Security** | ✅ | JWT, Admin/Operator roles, AuthGuard |

## ⚠️ Partial / Missing

| Phase | Gap |
|-------|-----|
| **18 – Docker** | Dockerfiles and compose exist; AI worker service commented out |
| **19 – Documentation** | README exists; API docs and architecture could be expanded |

---

## Main gaps

### 1. Detection persistence and WebSocket events

- `log_detection()` exists but is **never called** from the stream pipeline.
- Face detections are drawn on the stream but not:
  - Saved to the `Detection` table
  - Broadcast via WebSocket (`face_recognized`, `unknown_person_detected`)
- Object detections are not written to the `ObjectDetection` table.

### 2. Unknown face storage and clustering

- `UnknownFace` model and API exist.
- `unknown_clustering.py` (DBSCAN) exists.
- Unknown faces are not:
  - Saved as snapshots
  - Stored in `UnknownFace`
  - Assigned cluster IDs via clustering.

### 3. Stream API auth

- Stream start/stop endpoints use `CurrentUser = None`, so they are effectively unauthenticated.

---

## Direction

The project matches the plan in structure and most features. The main missing pieces are:

1. Wiring `log_detection()` into the stream pipeline so detections are persisted and broadcast.
2. Saving unknown faces and using clustering.
3. Persisting object detections.
4. Enforcing auth on stream endpoints.

If you want, I can outline concrete code changes to wire detection logging, unknown-face storage, and stream auth.

Here’s what was changed and how to verify it.

## Fixes applied

### 1. Hydration error

- **Cause:** AuthGuard rendered different content on server (no token) vs client (with token), causing a hydration mismatch.
- **Change:** AuthGuard now uses a `mounted` state so server and client render the same initial UI (loading spinner). After mount, it checks the token and either redirects or shows the page.

### 2. Photo upload

- **Backend:** Added `File()` to the upload endpoint so the file parameter is correctly bound.
- **Frontend:** Adjusted error handling and ensured `FormData` is sent correctly (no `Content-Type` override for multipart).
- **Layout:** Simplified the Users page layout to avoid invalid HTML nesting that could affect behavior.

### 3. Users page layout

- **Change:** Table and empty state are now both inside `CardContent` to fix structure and avoid hydration issues.

### 4. `train_faces.py`

- **Change:** `os` is now imported at the top of the file so `os.path.exists` works correctly.

---

## How to test

1. Restart backend and frontend: `./scripts/start-dev.sh`
2. Open `http://localhost:3000/users`
3. Confirm the hydration error is gone.
4. Register a user (name + email).
5. Click the upload icon for that user and choose a face photo.
6. Confirm the upload completes and the Face column shows “✓ Ready”.

---

## Still pending (from earlier audit)

- **Detection logging:** Wire `log_detection()` into the stream pipeline so detections are stored and broadcast via WebSocket.
- **Unknown face storage:** Save unknown faces and assign cluster IDs.
- **Object detection persistence:** Write object detections to the database.

If you want, I can implement these next.

Summary of changes:

## Done

### 1. Toast notifications
- Added `ToastContext` with MUI Snackbar
- Wired into `ThemeProvider`
- Used on: Users, Cameras, Live Monitoring, Login
- Methods: `toast()`, `success()`, `error()`, `info()`, `warning()`

### 2. Detection logging
- Added `detection_log_queue.py` for thread-safe detection events
- Detection overlay enqueues face detections (known/unknown)
- Background task processes queue and calls `log_detection()`
- Detections stored in DB and broadcast via WebSocket
- Throttled to 1 log per (camera_id, user_id) every 5 seconds

### 3. Docker and DB
- Added `/health/db` endpoint for DB connectivity
- Added `backend/scripts/pre-start.sh` to run migrations and seed admin
- Backend Dockerfile runs pre-start before uvicorn
- Updated README with Docker usage

### 4. Files changed
- `frontend/src/contexts/ToastContext.tsx` – new
- `frontend/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` – uses `ToastProvider`
- `frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/users/page.tsx` – toasts
- `frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/cameras/page.tsx` – toasts
- `frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/live/page.tsx` – toasts
- `frontend/src/app/login/page.tsx` – toasts
- `backend/app/services/detection_log_queue.py` – new
- `backend/app/services/detection_overlay.py` – enqueues detections
- `backend/app/services/stream_manager.py` – passes `camera_id` to overlay
- `backend/app/main.py` – DB health check, detection queue processor
- `backend/scripts/pre-start.sh` – new
- `docker/Dockerfile.backend` – runs migrations on startup
- `README.md` – Docker section updated

### 5. DB health check
```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/health/db
# {"status":"healthy","database":"connected"}
```

### 6. Docker usage
```bash
# Full stack
docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up -d

# DB only (dev)
docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.dev.yml up -d
```

If port 5432 is already in use (e.g. local PostgreSQL), either stop the local DB or change the port in `docker-compose.yml` before starting Docker.

[Debian -- Error](https://packages.debian.org/stable/libs/libgl1-mesa-glx)<br/><br/># libgl1-mesa-glx Replacement in Debian<br/><br/>**What happened:** `libgl1-mesa-glx` is now a transitional/dummy package in Debian and has been removed from newer releases.[2][4] It is no longer available as an installation candidate in recent Debian and Ubuntu versions (such as Debian Trixie and Ubuntu 23.10).[4]<br/><br/>**What it was replaced with:** The package has been superseded by its dependencies:[2][4]<br/>- `libgl1` (Vendor neutral GL dispatch library)<br/>- `libglx-mesa0` (free implementation of the OpenGL API -- GLX vendor library)<br/><br/>**When this changed:** This transition occurred at least since Debian Buster (2019), but the old transitional packages were completely removed in Debian Trixie and Ubuntu 23.10.[4]<br/><br/>**How to fix it:** If you need OpenGL support, install the replacement packages directly (`libgl1` and `libglx-mesa0`) instead of trying to install the deprecated `libgl1-mesa-glx` package.[4] |

Summary of changes:

## 1. Docker frontend build

**Cause:** `package-lock.json` was out of sync with `package.json`, so `npm ci` failed.

**Change:** Switched from `npm ci` to `npm install` in the frontend Dockerfile so the build works even when the lock file is outdated.

Rebuild with:

```bash
docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
```

---

## 2. Email in Users

**Current use of email:**
- **Unique identifier** – avoids duplicate users (same email cannot be registered twice)
- **Display** – shown in the Users table
- **Schema** – stored as `unique=True, nullable=False` in the DB

**Not used for:**
- Dashboard login (that uses `AuthUser`, which is separate)
- Face recognition (that uses face embeddings)

**Is it required?**  
Right now it is required. It can be made optional if you prefer another identifier (e.g. employee ID or just name). If you want that, say how you’d like to identify users instead.

_**User**_

You are a Senior AI Architect, Computer Vision Engineer, and Distributed Systems Engineer.

Build a production-grade AI Surveillance Platform capable of large-scale real-time face recognition and object detection across multiple cameras.

The system must use ONLY open-source technologies.

The system should be scalable enough to support 50+ cameras simultaneously.

Do NOT generate everything at once.
Build the system phase by phase.

SYSTEM CAPABILITIES

The platform must include:

Real-Time Face Recognition
Unknown Person Detection
Face Anti-Spoofing
Person Tracking Across Cameras
Object Detection
Multi-Camera Monitoring
Live Dashboard
AI Analytics
Alert System
Unknown Face Clustering
Vector Database Search
Distributed Camera Workers
GPU Acceleration

CORE TECHNOLOGIES

Backend

Python
FastAPI
OpenCV
NumPy
SQLAlchemy

Face Recognition

InsightFace

Vector Search

FAISS

Object Detection

YOLOv8

Person Tracking

DeepSORT

Message Queue

Redis or RabbitMQ

Database

PostgreSQL

Frontend

Next.js
React
TailwindCSS

Dashboard UI

minimals.cc

Deployment

Docker
Docker Compose

Optional GPU

CUDA

SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

Cameras
↓
Frame Capture Workers
↓
Frame Queue (Redis)
↓
AI Processing Pipeline
↓
Face Detection
↓
Face Embedding Extraction
↓
Vector Search (FAISS)
↓
Known / Unknown Classification
↓
Anti-Spoofing Check
↓
Person Tracking (DeepSORT)
↓
Object Detection
↓
Event Logger
↓
WebSocket Event Dispatcher
↓
Admin Dashboard

Each camera must run in an isolated worker.

ENTERPRISE PROJECT STRUCTURE

ai-surveillance-system

backend
│
├── app
│ ├── api
│ │ ├── auth.py

FACE RECOGNITION PIPELINE

For each frame:

Capture frame
Detect face
Align face
Generate embedding
Search embedding in FAISS
Compute cosine similarity
If similarity above threshold → Known
Else → Unknown

Draw bounding box.

Green box → Known
Red box → Unknown

ANTI-SPOOFING

Detect if face is:

Printed photo
Phone screen replay
Mask attack

Reject spoof attempts.

PERSON TRACKING

Use DeepSORT.

Track same individual across frames.

Each person gets unique tracking ID.

OBJECT DETECTION

Use YOLOv8.

Detect objects:

person
phone
laptop
bag
bottle
car
motorcycle
bicycle

Objects must also be logged.

REAL TIME DASHBOARD

Use WebSockets.

Dashboard features:

Live camera feeds
Real-time detections
Unknown face alerts
Object alerts
Detection analytics

PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION

Use:

FAISS vector search
GPU inference
Frame skipping
Batch embedding comparisons
Async pipelines

The system should support:

50+ cameras.

ALERT SYSTEM

Trigger alerts for:

Unknown person detected
Camera offline
Restricted object detected

Alerts must appear in dashboard.

SECURITY

Implement:

JWT authentication
Admin roles
Operator roles

DEPLOYMENT

Create Docker setup for:

Backend API
Frontend Dashboard
PostgreSQL Database
Redis Queue
AI Worker

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS

Generate:

Complete code
File-by-file implementation
Installation commands
Docker setup
Database migrations
README documentation

</tool-use>

Summary of what was implemented:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Minimal UI Theme (free.minimals.cc)**

- **`frontend/src/theme.ts`** – Minimal-style theme:
  - Primary: `#2065D1`, secondary: `#3366FF`
  - Success: `#00AB55`, warning: `#FFAB00`, error: `#FF5630`
  - Text: `#212B36`, `#637381`, `#919EAB`
  - Background: `#F9FAFB`
  - Card shadows, rounded corners (16px), scrollbar styling
  - MUI overrides for Card, Button, AppBar, Drawer, ListItemButton, Chip, TextField, Paper

- **`frontend/src/app/layout.tsx`** – Switched font from Inter to Public Sans

- **`frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx`** – Minimal layout:
  - White header with logout
  - Left sidebar with logo and nav
  - Nav items with selected state and hover styles

- **Pages** – Updated headers and typography:
  - Dashboard: “Hi, Welcome back” with stat cards
  - Login: Minimal card with icon
  - Live, Cameras, Users, Detections, Analytics, Alerts: consistent headings and descriptions

### 2. **FAISS Vector Search**

- **`backend/app/vector_db/faiss_index.py`** – FAISS index:
  - `IndexFlatIP` with L2-normalized vectors for cosine similarity
  - `rebuild()` from `(user_id, embedding)` pairs
  - `search()` and `search_batch()` with threshold
  - Fallback when FAISS is not installed

- **`backend/app/ai/face_matcher.py`** – Uses FAISS when available, otherwise linear scan

- **`backend/app/services/detection_overlay.py`** – Rebuilds FAISS index when embeddings are loaded (every 60s)

- **`backend/requirements.txt`** – Added `faiss-cpu>=1.8.0`

### 3. **Object Detection Logging**

- **`backend/app/services/logging_service.py`** – `log_object_detection()`:
  - Writes to `ObjectDetection`
  - Broadcasts `object_detected` over WebSocket

- **`backend/app/services/detection_log_queue.py`** – `enqueue_object_detection()` with throttling

- **`backend/app/services/detection_overlay.py`** – Enqueues object detections for each detected object

### 4. **Unknown Face Persistence**

- **`backend/app/services/detection_overlay.py`** – `_save_unknown_face_crop()`:
  - Saves face crops to `storage/unknown_faces/`
  - Filename: `unknown_{timestamp}_{camera_id}_{uuid}.jpg`

- **`backend/app/services/detection_log_queue.py`** – `_log_unknown_face()`:
  - Inserts into `UnknownFace` with `image`, `embedding`, `camera_id`

- **`enqueue_detection()`** – Extended with `snapshot_path` and `embedding` for unknown faces

### Next Steps

1. **Install FAISS** – `pip install faiss-cpu` in the backend environment.
2. **Create storage dirs** – Ensure `storage/unknown_faces` exists (or is created by the app).
3. **Run frontend** – `npm run dev` in the frontend directory to verify the Minimal UI.

The analytics API already uses `ObjectDetection` for object stats, so object logging will feed those charts automatically.

Summary of changes:

## Docker build fixes

### 1. **Backend Dockerfile** (`docker/Dockerfile.backend`)
- **Build tools**: Added `build-essential`, `g++`, `gcc` so InsightFace can compile its Cython extensions.
- **Torch CPU**: Install PyTorch CPU before other deps to avoid pulling CUDA (~3GB):
  ```dockerfile
  pip install torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu
  ```

### 2. **Pre-start script** (`backend/scripts/pre-start.sh`)
- **DB wait**: Retries DB connection up to 10 times (3s apart) before running migrations.
- **Logging**: Clear `[Visioryx]` messages for migrations, seed, and startup.

### 3. **Docker Compose**
- **Backend env**: Uses `backend/.env.docker` (always present) instead of optional `.env`.
- **Frontend build args**: `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL` passed at build time for VPS/production.

### 4. **`.env.docker`**
- Added `backend/.env.docker` with defaults so Docker runs without a custom `.env`.

---

## UI updates

- **Mobile nav**: Menu icon opens a temporary drawer on small screens.
- **Table styling**: Table headers and row hover styles aligned with Minimal UI.
- **Layout**: Shared nav content for desktop and mobile drawers.

---

## How to run

```bash
cd /path/to/Visioryx
docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up --build -d
```

Backend startup flow:
1. Wait for PostgreSQL
2. Run Alembic migrations
3. Seed admin (`admin@visioryx.dev` / `admin123`)
4. Start uvicorn on port 8000

---

## VPS deployment

For production, set build args when building the frontend:

```yaml
frontend:
  build:
    args:
      NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: https://api.yourdomain.com
      NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL: wss://api.yourdomain.com
```

And override `SECRET_KEY` in the backend environment.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Checking CORS policy**

I'm investigating potential CORS issues blocking the POST request to start the stream. The CORS policy should allow requests from localhost:3000 since it's in the allow_origins list. The frontend at localhost:3000 will request from localhost:8000, which should be allowed by CORS if both are in the allow_origins list. Img tags can handle MJPEG streams cross-origin, so CORS shouldn't block the stream. 

**Handling RTSP streams**

The RTSP stream capture fails silently if the URL is invalid or camera is unreachable, causing empty frames to be yielded. I found that empty frames cause no yield, leading to black screen issues. I identified multiple failure points in RTSP stream initialization: POST /start errors, incorrect streamUrl, null token issues, and stream opening failures. 

**Checking auth flow**

I'm verifying the auth flow and token handling in the API calls. The frontend's API calls route through `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` which defaults to `http://localhost:8000`, but image streams use the same base URL so may not respect cross-host auth properly. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Handling auth dependency**

The auth dependency is mandatory and will raise 401 if no token, so the frontend must send the Authorization header. The frontend fetches `/api/v1/stream/1/start` directly to `localhost:8000` instead of using the Next.js rewrite rule. 

The MJPEG endpoint uses JWT token in query params instead of auth header, so no auth required. 

**Testing connection**

I'm testing connection to localhost:8000 which should work since Docker exposes port 8000. The RTSP connection might fail because the camera is on the host network; using `network_mode: host` or `host.docker.internal` could resolve this. The RTSP connection fails because the camera is on the host network, requiring `network_mode: host` or `host.docker.internal`. 

**Validating stream URL**

I'm checking if the stream URL is malformed due to incorrect `base` value when `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is unset. The Next.js server proxies /api requests to http://localhost:8000, which should correctly route to the backend container in Docker. 

**Updating proxy config**

I'm changing the Next.js proxy config from `localhost:8000` to `backend:8000` for Docker environments. I'm updating the proxy config to use `backend:8000` for Docker environments. 

I'm checking if the rewrite rule needs to account for container networking differences between localhost:3000 and localhost:8000. I'm adding an environment variable for the API URL in the rewrite rules to handle Docker container networking differences. I'm switching to using NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL for direct backend requests to avoid Docker networking issues. I'm setting NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL to ensure streamUrl uses the correct backend origin instead of window.location.origin. 

**Debugging stream start**

I'm investigating why 'Start Stream' isn't working by checking potential issues: auth, routing, backend errors, CORS, or frontend updates. </details></think>
